title: The City of God, Volume I
author: Augustine, of Hippo, Saint
summary: Augustine answers pagan accusations that Christianity caused Rome's calamities, especially the sack by the Goths under Alaric. Prompted by Marcellinus and correspondence with Volusian, Augustine defends the faith, refutes claims that pagan worship secured worldly prosperity, and contends that calamities have always afflicted mankind. The work divides into two parts: ten books rebut pagan arguments about gods and happiness, and twelve books presenting Augustine's positive doctrine of the two cities (the City of God and the earthly city), their origins, progress, and final destinies, linking history to divine providence and Christian hope.

characters.name: Aurelius Augustine
characters.lifespan: Authors the work between 413 and 426; completes the City of God in maturity (described as his life-work)

characters.name: Marcellinus
characters.lifespan: Instrumental in urging Augustine to enlarge his defence; reported to have died shortly before the work began (died before/during early composition)

characters.name: Volusian
characters.personality: courteous, intellectually inquisitive, representative of cultured pagan statesmen
characters.lifespan: Correspondent of Augustine; sought answers to political and social difficulties about Christianity

characters.name: Honorius
characters.lifespan: Emperor who sent Marcellinus to Africa to settle ecclesiastical disputes; appears as historical referent

characters.name: Alaric
characters.lifespan: Leader of the Goths who sacked Rome; his sack of Rome is the immediate occasion for Augustine's defence

characters.name: Jerome
characters.lifespan: Quoted as lamenting Rome's fall with theatrical emotion; used to illustrate contemporary response

characters.name: Justin (Martyr)
characters.lifespan: Mentioned as a predecessor in Christian apologetics whose arguments are contrasted with Augustine's

characters.name: Tertullian
characters.lifespan: Mentioned among earlier apologists; Augustine's defence is compared to their work

characters.name: Varro
characters.personality: Roman scholar referenced for theological divisions
characters.lifespan: Referenced in the table of contents for discussion of his threefold division of theology

characters.name: Apuleius
characters.lifespan: Mentioned in context of doctrines about demons and mediators

characters.name: Porphyry
characters.lifespan: Referenced in discussion of doctrines of redemption

characters.name: Socrates
characters.lifespan: Mentioned in relation to Socratic philosophy in the text

characters.name: Plato
characters.lifespan: Referenced regarding Platonic philosophy discussed in the work

characters.name: Beugnot
characters.lifespan: Quoted as a modern critic who minimized the work's effect

characters.name: Volusian's friends (unnamed)
characters.lifespan: Participants in a conversation illustrating pagan doubts about Christianity

characters.name: Donatists
characters.lifespan: Their disputes prompted Marcellinus' mission to Africa, and led to contact with Augustine

characters.name: Marcus Dods
characters.lifespan: Editor of the translation presented in this edition

characters.name: George Wilson
characters.lifespan: Translator of selected books (IV, XVII, XVIII) in this edition

characters.name: J. J. Smith
characters.lifespan: Translator of books V–VIII in this edition

locations.name: Edinburgh, George Street (publisher location T. & T. Clark)
